Edwards' 30-point output in a 5-point road win is the canonical 'scoring-mode game' where AST volume collapses, and no current quant field captures this self-suppression dynamic. The MIN×AST suppressor only applies to opposing team's assist picks (the switching scheme suppresses opponents' ball-handlers), not MIN's own players. The playoff AST elevator (+23pp) appears to be derived from games where MIN wins comfortably — in tight road games, the directional pattern may reverse. STEP 4: 10/10 hit rate display at the base T2 tier — but this measure is at T2, not T4. The 78% confidence at T4 was already an interpolation below the confirmed tier. STEP 5: Tier walk correctly identified T6 as risky (overall window uncertain) and landed at T4 with H2H 4/4 (100%) as confirmation. The tier walk was procedurally sound. STEP 6: No lineup_update. This is model_gap_signal: no quant field currently exists for 'star scoring-mode compression' in close road playoff games — the condition where a player's scoring role intensifies at the direct expense of assist volume in a tight-margin game that requires them to carry the offense. The system would need a new signal — perhaps competitive_game_isolation_rate or road_close_game_AST_delta — to capture this structural pattern.

parlay_results.parlay_summary: Clean 2/2 sweep — Randle REB T6 hit 9 actual (STRONG-edge leg delivered) and Mobley AST T2 hit exactly at threshold (2 actual = 2 pick, graded HIT per exact-threshold rule), validating the independent multi-game anchor structure.
parlay_lessons: The Mobley AST T2 leg hit exactly at threshold (2 actual = 2 pick) — a legitimate HIT per grading rules, but this exact-threshold result reveals that the leg had essentially zero cushion. When building parlays anchored by a conservative 'floor' leg like Mobley AST T2, the structural risk is that any single possession change (one missed feed, one early bench-rest minute) flips a HIT to a MISS with no buffer. Future parlays should prefer legs with projected actual values ≥ 1.5x the pick_value when the leg's confidence is ≤ 78% and iron_floor is absent — a true 'anchor' leg needs cushion, not just a qualified floor.

The STRONG-edge Randle REB T6 leg (19.25pp edge, kelly_quarter=0.1034) functioned exactly as designed: it carried genuine positive expected value and delivered 9 actual vs 6 pick. Using a STRONG-rated single leg as the parlay anchor — a leg that independently justifies action on its own merit — is the correct construction pattern. Parlays built around legs with positive edge (calibrated_prob > market_implied_prob) are structurally superior to parlays built around high-hit-rate low-edge legs, because positive edge compounds rather than erodes the parlay's expected return.
parlay_reinforcements: Independent multi-game parlay construction (legs from DEN vs MIN and CLE vs TOR with zero correlated failure risk) worked cleanly — when a CLE blowout scenario could have compressed Mobley's late-game touches (and partially did), the Randle leg from a separate game was unaffected. Multi-game parlay independence is the strongest structural protection against correlated failure; this session validates the design principle.

Anchoring a parlay with a STRONG-edge REB leg (Randle T6, 19.25pp positive edge) alongside a conservative independent AST leg (Mobley T2, near-floor) produced a +113 implied odds combination that captured genuine value without overreaching on tier selection. The combination pattern — one positive-edge value leg + one conservative floor leg from a different game — is the optimal core parlay template: the value leg drives the payout, the floor leg provides structural certainty.

reinforcements: AST props across all contexts went 6/6 — the floor-lock strategy at T2/T4 with confirmed H2H patterns (Maxey 5/5, White 5/5, Pritchard 5/5, Edgecombe 5/5, Fox 3/4, Thompson 4/4) continues to be the system's most reliable prop type in playoff series; the series-sample H2H confirmation adds meaningful signal beyond season-wide rates.

REB picks went 2/2 (Jabari Smith 100%, Thompson 85%) — consistent-tagged rebounders at conservative tiers (T4) with confirmed minutes projection (proj_min=42) hit cleanly even against a LAL suppressor environment; the cautious tier selection below the tested H2H ceiling (T6 available, T4 selected) provided the safety margin.

Underdog scorer logic worked: Maxey PTS T15 (actual 29) and Edgecombe PTS T10 (actual 30) both hit comfortably despite BOS being a -14.5 favorite — the correct application of blowout caps exclusively to the favored team's players, not the underdog, preserved two strong picks that lesser logic might have suppressed.

Tier stepping on FG_COLD and FG_MARGIN_NEG was vindicated across multiple hits: Fox PTS stepped T15→T10 (actual 17, hit), Sengun PTS stepped T15→T10 (actual 20, hit), Thompson PTS stepped T15→T10 (actual 16, hit) — mandatory step-down rules consistently protected the system from overreach on shooting-cold or thin-margin scorers.

Fox AST T4 (actual 4, exact threshold) confirms the grading rule — exact-threshold results are HITs — and validates conservative tier selection for a primary PG (8 AST in G1, 9/10 overall) in a blowout-risk home game where the 80% spread cap was applied.
lessons: 3PM props are the clear weak link at 50% (2/4): both misses — Vassell and Wembanyama — went 0-for from three in a game that POR won by 3 (CLOSE). The close game script eliminated the primary risk factor the tier-walk cited (blowout Q4 rest), meaning the misses were pure cold-shooting variance, but the broader lesson is that 3PM T1 OVER picks at market-implied probabilities of 93-94% represent a systematic edge mismatch: the system's 80%-capped confidence is materially below market, and the market's pricing implies these are near-certain outcomes that occasionally fail on off-nights.

Wembanyama's injury exit (concussion, Q2, 12 minutes played) caused both his PTS T20 miss and his 3PM T1 miss — confirmed post_game_event = injury_exit. Both picks were sound at selection time with no pre-game injury flag (NOT_LISTED at check). The lesson is not analytical; it is operational: any pick on a player with documented physical vulnerability (Wemby was managing right ankle pain and had played only 4 games in the prior month after a rib contusion) should receive a pre-game injury-flag check that elevates a recent-return/multi-injury-concern player to a softer scrutiny tier, even when officially not listed.

Pritchard PTS T10 missed (actual 4, shot 2-8 / 0-4 3PT in 27 minutes) in a COMPETITIVE game (PHI won by 14) — the season H2H signal of 2/4 at T10 against PHI was a legitimate divergence from the 90% overall rate, and the tier-walk noted this concern but proceeded at 74%. The miss validates the principle: when season H2H hit rate vs. a specific opponent is ≤50% and the game is confirmed COMPETITIVE (not a blowout that compresses minutes), that divergence is a stronger signal than overall hit rate. A 50% H2H should trigger a skip or step-down to T5, not just a -3pp confidence penalty.
recommendations: Implement a hard ceiling on 3PM T1 OVER picks when market-implied probability exceeds 90%: if the market prices 1+ three-pointers at 93%+, the system's 80%-capped confidence creates a structural FADE, not a selection. Do not select 3PM T1 OVER when market_implied_prob > 90% unless the pick has a positive calibrated edge — use the bet_recommendation tier as a gate, not just confidence_pct.

Introduce a multi-injury-concern flag for players returning from injury within 10 games AND managing a concurrent physical issue: Wembanyama qualified (rib contusion April 6, right ankle soreness, only 4 games since return) but was NOT_LISTED at check. For such players, apply a structural -10pp confidence penalty on all props regardless of injury_status_at_check, and do not include them as anchor legs in parlays.

When season H2H hit rate vs. a specific series opponent is ≤50% at the selected tier (as with Pritchard PTS T10, 2/4 vs PHI), require the H2H rate to be ≥70% OR the series G1 result to show the tier cleared by ≥5 points before proceeding — a single series game hit does not override a consistent season-long H2H pattern of underperformance against that opponent.

For Wembanyama PTS props specifically: the vs_tough=64% at T20 was a material warning signal that the tier-walk noted but absorbed with only a -5pp deduction. In future games, when vs_tough drops >15pp below overall hit rate at the selected tier, treat this as a mandatory one-tier step-down (not just a confidence penalty), yielding T15 at higher confidence rather than T20 at reduced confidence.

Strengthen the 3PM parlay construction rule: two 3PM legs from different games (Wemby 3PM + Jabari Smith 3PM) both had market-implied probabilities >70% individually, but combined into a parlay with implied odds of +117 — this represents a low-juice combination where individual cold-shooting nights (especially from a player with a recent injury concern) can collapse the parlay. Require at least one non-3PM leg in any two-leg parlay, or require at least one iron_floor-tagged leg when both legs are 3PM props.

miss_details.root_cause: In-game injury exit confirmed by post-game news and pick object post_game_event field — Wembanyama suffered a concussion after slamming his jaw into the hardwood following a Jrue Holiday foul in Q2, played only 12 minutes, and entered the NBA concussion protocol. Player was NOT_LISTED at injury check (active at pick time); miss is entirely attributable to an unforeseeable in-game injury, not to pick selection logic. No amendment was triggered pre-game. The 9/10 hit rate display and tier walk to T20 were sound; the outcome was determined by an acute injury event.

In-game injury exit confirmed by post-game news and pick object post_game_event field — same concussion event as PTS miss above (12 minutes played, Q2 exit). The 3PM T1 pick had a 9/10 hit rate display and 95% overall rate; under normal game conditions (34+ minutes) this tier clears in the overwhelming majority of outcomes. The zero 3PM result is entirely explained by the early exit, not by cold shooting or analytical error. No amendment was triggered pre-game. Both Wembanyama misses share the same injury_event root cause and are structurally independent of pick selection quality.

Game context (STEP 0): POR @ SAS — POR won 106-103, a CLOSE game (3-point margin). No blowout game-script effects apply; Vassell played 34 full minutes confirming no restriction. Post-game news confirms cold shooting from deep (0-for on 3-pointers) with 16 points and 12 rebounds — a full, unrestricted game. The 9/10 hit rate display and iron_floor at T1 with a 90% overall rate represented a sound, well-supported pick. The tier walk correctly identified T1 at 90% overall (consistent tag), stepped down from T2 appropriately due to small H2H sample, and applied the spread cap (80% ceiling). The CLOSE game script is the key observation: the human_verdict trim rationale (Q4 rest in a blowout) was precisely wrong — SAS did not blow out POR, meaning Vassell played full minutes but simply went cold from three. This is a low-frequency cold-shooting event on a 9/10 pick; the quant signal was valid, the pick construction was sound, and the outcome falls within normal variance. No amendment was triggered.

Game context (STEP 0): PHI @ BOS — PHI won 111-97, a COMPETITIVE game (14-point margin). Game script is a relevant partial factor: PHI won outright, meaning BOS trailed in periods and the 'BOS cruises, Pritchard gets garbage time' scenario did not materialize — instead the reverse occurred, with BOS trailing and Pritchard's minutes (27) being compressed by lineup adjustments to chase the deficit. Post-game news confirms he shot 2-8 / 0-4 from three (4 points in 27 minutes) with no injury — pure cold shooting and reduced opportunity in a game where BOS was losing. The tier-walk noted the season H2H concern explicitly (2/4 at T10 vs PHI, 50%), flagged it as 'concerning,' and applied a -3pp penalty — but did not step down from T10 or skip. This is a model_gap_rule miss: the signal was present (50% season H2H at the selected tier is a disqualifying rate by the system's own 70% standard), the rule existed (H2H divergence from overall rate warrants step-down or skip), but the rule's implementation allowed a -3pp penalty to substitute for the more appropriate mandatory step-down. The 9/10 overall hit rate display masked the specific matchup weakness. The PHI COMPETITIVE win (outright upset) compounded the issue: Pritchard's minutes were compressed not by BOS blowout-resting him, but by BOS needing to chase the deficit with its primary rotation. No amendment was triggered. Tier-walk noted the H2H concern correctly but the rule response was insufficient.

parlay_results.parlay_summary: 0/1 — the Wembanyama 3PM leg failed due to a Q2 concussion injury exit (12 minutes played), collapsing the parlay despite the Jabari Smith 3PM leg hitting cleanly (3 made threes).
parlay_lessons: Do not anchor a parlay on a player with documented multi-injury vulnerability (Wembanyama: rib contusion April 6, right ankle soreness, recent-return status) even when officially NOT_LISTED at injury check — an acute in-game injury on a player already managing multiple physical concerns is a structurally elevated risk that no pre-game injury status captures, and using such a player as a parlay leg exposes the entire card to a single-point-of-failure that is correlated with known physical fragility, not independent variance.

Two-leg parlays built entirely on 3PM props (Wembanyama 3PM T1 + Jabari Smith 3PM T2) represent a low-juice construction (+117 implied) where cold-shooting variance on either leg collapses the card — require at least one iron_floor non-3PM leg in any two-leg parlay, or require that both legs have positive calibrated edge (bet_recommendation = STRONG or POSITIVE) before combining; in this parlay, Wembanyama 3PM had a NEUTRAL bet recommendation (calibrated_edge = -1.85%) and should not have been used as a parlay anchor.

When the cannibalization_flags field is null on a missed parlay (as here), the failure was not a structural H33 cannibalization error but rather a single-leg injury_event — reinforce that parlay construction should still treat any leg with FADE or NEUTRAL bet_recommendation tier as ineligible for multi-leg combination, regardless of the analytical confidence_pct assigned.
parlay_reinforcements: The Jabari Smith 3PM T2 leg (STRONG bet recommendation, +14.91pp calibrated edge) performed exactly as the selection logic intended — trend=up, 4/4 H2H vs LAL, actual 3 threes — confirming that STRONG-edge legs with confirmed H2H patterns and positive trend are the correct structural anchors for multi-leg parlays; the failure of this parlay was entirely due to the co-leg, not this leg's construction.

Cross-game multi-leg parlays (SAS game + HOU/LAL game) with genuinely independent correlation structures remain the correct parlay construction philosophy — the two legs had zero game-script correlation, meaning the LAL defensive performance had no bearing on SAS's 3PM output; the independence assumption was valid, and this structure should be preserved in future parlay construction while improving leg-quality gates.

reinforcements: SGA PTS T20 iron-floor directive validated again: despite a 5-18 FG shooting night in G1, SGA dropped 37 points in G2 — the structural streak floor is functioning exactly as designed. The FG_MARGIN_NEG step-down from T25 to T20 was the correct conservative move and produced a massive hit (37 actual vs 20 threshold). Continue treating T20 as a pre-qualified lock requiring zero evaluation tokens.

Blowout-resilient PHX underdog picks (Green PTS T15, Booker PTS T15) both hit decisively in a 13-point OKC win. Designating underdog players as exempt from blowout caps is correct — losing-side starters play full minutes in competitive/close games and PHX's scorers (Green 21, Booker 22) performed well. The blowout_games split in the quant context was the key supporting signal and it held.

The DET×AST suppressor correctly identified risk on Ausar Thompson AST T2 — Thompson posted only 1 AST in 28 minutes in G2, identical to his G1 result. The pattern of serial misses (G1: 1 AST, G2: 1 AST) against ORL constitutes a within-series suppressor signal that validates the pre-existing DET×AST suppressor. The suppressor framework is functioning correctly for wing/secondary playmakers.

Banchero REB T4 as a 'consistent'-tagged prop with proj_min=37 and a playoff REB boost hit cleanly (6 actual vs 4 threshold). The 95% per-player calibration rate for Banchero combined with the conservative T4 tier selection (stepping back from T6 due to offensive-first strictness rule) reflects sound tier discipline. High-consistency REB props for playoff starters with confirmed minutes are a reliable pick category.

The FG_MARGIN step-down cascade (T20→T15 for Banchero PTS, T20→T15 for Booker PTS, T25→T20 for SGA PTS) produced three correct hits with meaningful cushion — all three players cleared their stepped-down tiers by large margins (Banchero 18 vs 15, Booker 22 vs 15, SGA 37 vs 20). The mandatory step-down rule is functioning as a conservative safety net without sacrificing hit rate.
lessons: Ausar Thompson AST T2 missed for the second consecutive game (G1: 1 AST, G2: 1 AST). The pick reasoning correctly identified that the DET×AST suppressor targets ORL opponents, not DET players — but failed to account for a separate mechanism: Thompson is a wing/secondary playmaker whose AST production in half-court playoff basketball against a defense that clamps his creation (ORL is a confirmed tough defensive opponent) is structurally low. The 8/10 historical hit rate may be inflated by regular-season games against weaker defenses. In playoff series against ORL specifically, Thompson's AST role is compressed regardless of which direction the suppressor points. The series now shows 2 consecutive 1-AST games — this is a within-series signal, not variance.

The DET×AST suppressor directional framing needs clarification: the suppressor describes DET's defensive scheme suppressing opponents' AST, but the data now suggests DET's offensive system may also compress secondary-creator AST for DET players themselves in playoff half-court environments. Thompson's 8/10 regular-season rate is not predictive of playoff series performance against disciplined defenses. Future AST picks for DET secondary creators (non-Cunningham) in playoff series should carry an additional -5pp caution.

Jalen Williams PTS T10 hit (19 actual) but the pick deserved the manual_skip human verdict it received. The post_game_event confirms he exited with a left hamstring injury in Q3 — had the threshold been higher (T15 pre-step) or had he exited earlier, this would have been a miss. The system correctly stepped down via blowout_secondary_scorer logic, but the human auditor correctly identified the structural fragility. A secondary scorer with a volatile profile, no iron_floor, and a blowout spread ≥16.5 should be skipped entirely at T10 as well as T15 when historical blowout rest patterns for non-primary scorers are in play.
recommendations: Implement a within-series AST suppression rule: if a player has missed the AST threshold in the immediately preceding game(s) of the same playoff series, apply an additional -7pp confidence penalty to their next AST pick regardless of season hit rate. Thompson's two consecutive 1-AST games vs ORL in this series would have triggered this rule in G2 — the regular-season 8/10 rate is not series-specific and overestimates reliability in a settled defensive matchup.

Add a 'DET secondary creator AST caution' flag mirroring the suppressor logic for ORL opponents: for any DET non-primary playmaker (raw_avgs AST < 4.0) in playoff games, apply -5pp to AST confidence and require a minimum 9/10 hit rate before selecting T2. Thompson's series data (0-for-2 at T2 in this series) demonstrates the half-court playoff environment compresses his creation regardless of opponent identity.

For secondary scorers on the favored team in games with spread_abs ≥ 15 and no iron_floor, enforce a hard skip at ALL PTS tiers above T10 AND skip T10 as well when the player has a VOLATILE tag OR post_miss_hr < 70%. Jalen Williams at T10 hit this time but exited injured — the structural risk (blowout rest + minutes compression + hamstring history) was real and the system was fortunate. The human manual_skip verdict was correct; codify it as an automatic rule.

When a player's raw_avg for a prop is at or below the pick_value (e.g. Booker raw_avg REB 3.8 at T4 REB), require either an iron_floor tag OR a minimum 9/10 H2H hit rate AND a minimum 9/10 overall hit rate before selecting. Booker REB T4 hit this session but the trim_reason correctly flagged that season-average performance misses the line. This pick is structurally dependent on small H2H sample and playoff boost adjuster — not a sustainable selection without additional structural support.

Revisit the 70% minimum confidence floor for AST picks in playoff games: with 4 AST picks this session at 70-74% confidence producing only 3/4 (75%), and the lone miss being the minimum-confidence Thompson pick, consider raising the minimum floor for playoff AST props to 74% (eliminating 70-73% AST picks entirely). The Thompson miss followed an identical G1 miss — both at minimum confidence. The 70% floor appears too permissive for the reduced-possession, half-court playoff environment where assist generation is inherently less predictable.

miss_details.root_cause: STEP 0 — GAME CONTEXT: ORL @ DET final 83-98, DET won by 15. COMPETITIVE game script — margin 10-19 points. Game script is not a causal blowout factor. STEP 1 — ACTIVITY: Thompson played 28 confirmed minutes (post-game news confirmed), scored 11 points but recorded only 1 assist. Player was fully active; miss is stat-specific. STEP 2 — CLASSIFICATION: No injury exit noted. No workflow gap. Player hit rate was 8/10 (80%) at T2. The miss is classified as variance — the 8/10 hit rate implies roughly a 20% miss probability and this outcome falls within that range. However, this is the second consecutive game (G1: 1 AST, G2: 1 AST) that Thompson has posted only 1 assist vs ORL in this series, creating a within-series signal that warrants a rule adjustment going forward. STEP 3 — REASONING CRITIQUE: The reasoning correctly identified that the DET×AST suppressor targets ORL opponents' assist generation, not DET players' assists. This framing was technically accurate but missed a separate mechanism: Thompson as a wing secondary creator (raw_avgs AST ~2-3) faces compressed playmaking opportunities in playoff half-court basketball where defenses prioritize eliminating secondary creation. The pick relied on the 8/10 regular-season rate without accounting for the playoff context degrading that rate. STEP 4 — HIT RATE: 8/10 overall is a legitimate sample supporting the pick. The miss is the 2-in-10 outcome. However, the within-series pattern (0-for-2 in this specific matchup) suggests opponent-specific suppression beyond what the regular-season sample captured. STEP 5 — TIER WALK: T4 was correctly hard-gated (raw_avgs AST < 4.0 + tough opponent). T2 was the only viable tier. Single-tier evaluation was structurally forced, not an analytical error — flagged as null. STEP 6 — AMENDMENT: No lineup_update sub-object present for this pick. The afternoon amendment agent found no lineup change to trigger a revision. The human_verdict was 'trim' with correct reasoning citing ORL's tightened defense (def_recency tough, -10.96% L5 delta) — the trim signal was prescient but the pick ran. This is the second consecutive miss on Thompson AST T2 in this series — a within-series pattern that should trigger a suppression rule for subsequent games.

parlay_results.parlay_summary: Clean 1/1 — ORL-PHX Anchor parlay swept both REB legs with margin (Banchero 6 REB vs 4 threshold, Booker 7 REB vs 4 threshold), validating the two-game independent REB construction at +101 implied odds.

reinforcements: REB props went 6/6 (100%) — the tier-walking discipline of applying 25th-pct gates, stepping from T6→T4 when the gate fires, and respecting iron_floor tags produced a clean sweep across Mobley, Barnes, Randle, Johnson, and Anunoby. Continue applying this structured floor logic on all REB picks.

76-80% confidence band outperformed at 85.7% actual vs 78.0% expected — the higher-conviction picks (Harden 3PM T2, Barnes PTS/REB/AST, Jokic PTS/REB) were well-calibrated and delivered. This band is performing above expectation; continue prioritizing well-supported picks in the 76-80% range.

The desperate home context read for TOR was validated: Barnes 33 PTS/5 REB/11 AST and Barrett 33 PTS/5 AST both significantly exceeded their pick tiers. The 'home underdog desperate context maximizes usage' heuristic was correct and earned large cushions on T10 and T15 PTS picks.

Jokic PTS T20 iron_floor held despite the DEN blowout loss (27 PTS actual vs 20 pick) and despite a lineup_update flagging downside. The iron_floor tier protection rule correctly prevented a step-down that would have been overcautious. Amendment was overcautious: revised down to 70% but pick hit at 80%.

CJ McCollum 3PM iron_floor T1 (top pick) hit 2/2 threes in a close 1-point ATL win — the iron_floor designation on a trend=up shooter at T1 with H2H 3/3 was structurally sound and delivered even in a game where his AST prop missed badly (1 AST). Iron_floor on 3PM correctly decoupled the shooting prop from assist-role compression.
lessons: AST props went 6/10 (60%) — the weakest prop type on the card. Three of four AST misses occurred in games where game-script (MIN blowout win, TOR home close game) either compressed or disrupted passing sequences. The system's AST evaluation over-relied on H2H and series rates without adequately weighting within-game role compression in blowout environments. Tighten AST selection to require iron_floor or 9/10 hit rate in any game with spread_abs ≥ 8.

Jokic AST T8 missed badly (3 actual vs 8 pick) in a blowout loss (DEN lost by 17). The DEN@MIN game was COMPETITIVE (17-point margin, classified borderline blowout). The VOLATILE penalty and MIN×AST suppressor were both active, and the lineup_update correctly flagged downside (Gordon OUT, revised to 67%). The pick ran at 70% despite the amendment signal. Rule fix: when lineup_update direction=down revises confidence below the prop-type floor (68% for AST), the pick should be voided or stepped down further — do not run AST picks when the amendment drops confidence below the floor.

Donovan Mitchell PTS T20 missed (15 actual vs 20 pick) in a TOR blowout win (TOR won by 22 — BLOWOUT). Mitchell had a pre-game ankle concern (listed questionable) and the game went to a 22-point TOR margin. The blowout game-script suppressed his scoring in the second half as CLE trailed badly. The VOLATILE tag and FG_MARGIN_THIN:4% step-down from T25 to T20 were correctly applied, but the blowout risk from CLE being a road underdog in a must-win TOR home game was not sufficiently penalized. Rule fix: when a road team is in a desperate must-win road context (down 0-2 in series) against a home team with home-crowd energy, apply an additional blowout-risk adjustment to PTS props for the road team's star scorer.

Jamal Murray PTS T20 missed (16 actual vs 20 pick) in the same DEN blowout loss (DEN lost 96-113, COMPETITIVE/borderline blowout). FG_COLD:-9% was noted but classified as 'below 15% threshold' and not actioned. Murray scored 16 in 35 minutes — the blowout script (DEN lost by 17) compressed his second-half production. The amendment correctly flagged upside (Jokic OUT → revised to 85%) but the pick still missed because the blowout suppressed Murray's floor despite elevated usage. The lesson: usage redistribution from a star's absence does not override game-script blowout suppression — if the team loses badly, the secondary star's counting stats are still compressed by garbage-time. Do not upgrade PTS confidence on absence-driven redistribution when the game is already trending toward a blowout.

Mikal Bridges 3PM T1 missed (0 actual, 21 minutes played). The human verdict was 'trim' citing VOLATILE and raw_avg_3PM of only 1.4. The post-game news confirms he played only 21 minutes with 0-for-2 from three — reduced role via coach's decision in a close game. With raw_avg_3PM of 1.4 and no iron_floor, a non-iron-floor 3PM T1 pick for a player with documented VOLATILE tag and only 21 projected/actual minutes is structurally too thin. Rule fix: for non-iron-floor 3PM T1 picks where raw_avg_3PM < 1.5, require iron_floor or skip entirely — the distribution is too wide to reliably clear T1.

CJ McCollum AST T2 missed (1 actual vs 2 pick) despite 9/10 overall hit rate and consistent tag. He played 35 full minutes and scored 23 points as the hero of a 1-point ATL win — a scoring-mode hero game where he did not distribute. The tier_walk correctly identified T2 as a structural near-certain floor, but in a close game where McCollum was the go-to scorer (23 PTS, hero role), his assist generation was suppressed by isolation usage. Model gap: the system does not currently flag hero-scorer role compression risk on AST props for non-primary playmakers in close-game crunch-time contexts. This is a model_gap_signal — no quant field captures 'player will enter hero-scorer isolation mode in a 1-point game.'
recommendations: Implement a blowout-road-underdog PTS cap: when a player's team is (a) on the road, (b) down 2-0 or 0-2 in a playoff series (desperate context for the HOME team, not the road team), and (c) the road team is a confirmed underdog by ≥4 points, apply a mandatory -5pp confidence penalty to all PTS picks for the road team's primary scorers and do not select T20+ PTS picks without iron_floor. This would have prevented or flagged the Mitchell PTS T20 miss.

Add a lineup_update veto rule for AST props: when the afternoon lineup agent issues a direction=down amendment that revises AST confidence below the prop-type floor (68%), automatically void the AST pick rather than running it at the floor. The Jokic AST T8 pick ran at 70% despite the amendment dropping it to 67% — this is an execution gap. The amendment agent is a downstream check; its below-floor revisions must trigger auto-void.

For non-iron-floor 3PM T1 picks: require raw_avg_3PM ≥ 2.0 OR iron_floor = true before selection. Players with raw_avg_3PM between 1.0–1.9 and no iron_floor should be skipped at T1 regardless of overall hit rate, because a single 0-for-2 or 0-for-3 shooting night (entirely within normal variance for a 1.4 avg player) collapses the prop. Mikal Bridges (1.4 raw avg, VOLATILE, 21 minutes) is the prototype case.

Do not boost PTS confidence for absence-driven usage redistribution when pre-game signals include FG_COLD or VOLATILE and the opponent is a top-6 defensive team. The Jamal Murray amendment flagged 'upside revised to 85%' due to Jokic OUT, but Murray's FG_COLD:-9% + VOLATILE + DEN losing by 17 produced a 16-point output. Absence-driven upside revisions should be capped at +5pp maximum for VOLATILE PTS props and should not override FG_COLD signals already present at pick time.

Flag hero-scorer AST compression risk for non-primary playmakers (raw_avg_AST < 5.0) in close-game contexts: when a player has a PTS/AST ratio > 5:1 in their recent L5 games AND the game spread is < 4 points (close game likely), apply a mandatory -5pp penalty to their AST props and do not select AST T2+ without iron_floor. CJ McCollum (23 PTS, 1 AST in a 1-point game) and Jalen Brunson (26 PTS, 4 AST in a 1-point game) both exhibited this pattern on the same slate — two independent data points suggesting the signal is real.

miss_details.root_cause: STEP 0: NYK@ATL — ATL won by 1 (CLOSE game). No blowout effect. STEP 1: Brunson played 40 minutes and scored 26 PTS — active, full performance, not a DNP or injury event. STEP 2: injury_status_at_check = NOT_LISTED; post-game news confirms full minutes, no injury. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'series 2/2 at T6 and road ATL split 9 APG.' The series data was sound, but in a 1-point game Brunson entered scoring-carry mode (26 PTS) which suppressed his distribution — this is a known trade-off between PTS and AST in close games not captured by the split data. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 8/10 overall but vs_tough dropped to 60% — analyst correctly identified this as a downgrade risk and reduced confidence to 70%. STEP 5: Tier walk showed vs_tough=60% as a severe drop; the analyst applied the penalty. STEP 6: No lineup_update present. The pick was structurally sound at 70% with the vs_tough penalty already absorbed; Brunson's 26-PTS/4-AST output in a 1-point game reflects hero-scorer isolation mode suppressing passing in crunch time — within the expected variance distribution for a 70% pick.

STEP 0: NYK@ATL — ATL won by 1 (CLOSE game). No blowout effect on NYK starters, but close game context means reduced predictable shot distribution. STEP 1: Bridges played only 21 minutes (confirmed post-game news), went 0-for-2 from three. Reduced minutes reflect a coach's decision in a close game, not an injury. STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; no injury exit. The reduced role (21 min vs ~32+ projected) was a coaching decision in a close game. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'T1 3PM floor with 8/10 overall and stable trend; ATL lacks elite perimeter defenders.' This missed the key risk that Brunson's scoring dominance in a close road game compresses secondary-scorer minutes and shot opportunities for complementary players like Bridges. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 8/10 (80% base) was sound, but the VOLATILE tag on a player with raw_avg_3PM ~1.4 means the distribution is wide enough that 0-for-2 (0 makes) is a normal outcome. Human verdict correctly flagged 'trim.' STEP 5: Tier walk correctly identified T1 as the only viable tier. STEP 6: No lineup_update. The model gap: no rule exists to penalize non-iron-floor 3PM T1 picks when raw_avg_3PM < 1.5 and VOLATILE is tagged — the rule should require iron_floor or skip for thin-average 3PM shooters. With 21 actual minutes (vs ~32 projected), minutes suppression was also a factor not predicted by the tier walk.

STEP 0: NYK@ATL — ATL won by 1 (CLOSE game). No blowout. STEP 1: McCollum played 35 full minutes, scored 23 PTS — the hero of the win. Active and healthy. STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; post-game confirms full minutes with no injury. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited '95% overall AST with consistent tag; series-new but structural floor is near-certain at T2.' This was correct on the historical data but missed the contextual risk: in a 1-point game where McCollum was ATL's go-to scorer (23 PTS, hero role), his usage shifted entirely to isolation scoring and he simply did not generate assist opportunities. The T2 near-certainty assumption broke down under hero-scorer crunch-time isolation mode. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 9/10 (95% overall, consistent tag) — this is a genuine high-quality miss on an ordinarily reliable prop. STEP 5: Tier walk correctly identified T2 as near-certain based on structural data. The miss was not predictable from any available quant field. STEP 6: No lineup_update. Classification: model_gap_signal — the system has no quant field that captures hero-scorer role compression in close-game crunch-time contexts for non-primary playmakers. McCollum's PTS/AST ratio in L5 games and the game's close spread were available at pick time but no rule connects them to AST suppression risk. This is a genuine missing signal.

STEP 0: NYK@ATL — ATL won by 1 (CLOSE game). No blowout. STEP 1: Alexander-Walker played 38 minutes and scored 14 PTS — active, full game, near-threshold output. STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; post-game confirms no injury exit, full minutes. The miss was by 1 point (14 actual vs 15 pick). STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'consistent 90% tag at T15; home G3 returns familiar environment after away G2 miss.' The home game context was correct (he played 38 minutes in a competitive close win), but the within-series suppression signal (G2 miss: 9 pts) was real and the analyst appropriately noted it. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 9/10 overall (90%), but series showed a G2 miss at 9 pts and analyst correctly applied within-series penalty to reduce confidence to 74%. vs_tough=82% meant NYK's tough defense was a 8pp drag. STEP 5: Tier walk showed T15 as the starting tier with no step-down needed (no FG_MARGIN flag). The tier was correct. STEP 6: No lineup_update. With a 14-point actual output against a 15-point pick (a 1-point miss) and the analyst already applying within-series and tough-defense penalties to reach 74%, this is within normal variance for a 74% confidence pick. The NYK defense held Alexander-Walker 1 point under threshold in a 1-point game — no structural failure.

STEP 0: CLE@TOR — TOR won by 22 (BLOWOUT). TOR won at home by 22 points. Mitchell was on the LOSING side of a blowout road game. STEP 1: Mitchell played 33 minutes (confirmed post-game news), scored 15 PTS — active but limited by blowout game script. STEP 2: Post-game news notes Mitchell was listed questionable pre-game with a right ankle injury but played 33 minutes. Not an injury exit. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'FG_MARGIN step from T25 to T20; playoff +3pp boost and series 2/2 at T25 support the floor.' The reasoning did not account for the TOR home desperate context — TOR was down 0-2 and playing at home, which maximized their intensity and elevated the blowout risk for CLE. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 8/10 overall. The VOLATILE tag and FG_MARGIN_THIN:4% step were correctly applied. Mitchell profile carries 'CAUTION PTS' designation. STEP 5: Tier walk correctly stepped T25→T20 on FG_MARGIN. At T20 the hit rate was 80%, but the blowout game-script (TOR won by 22) suppressed Mitchell's second-half production. STEP 6: lineup_update direction=unchanged (Quickley OUT had no noted impact on Mitchell PTS). Model gap rule: the system applied the CAUTION PTS flag but lacked a specific rule to add blowout-risk penalty when the road team star is facing a desperate home team that is down 0-2 in a series. The 22-point blowout was predictable from the TOR home must-win desperation context but no rule enforced a spread-expansion penalty for this scenario. Amendment correctly found no revision warranted — the gap was in pre-pick rule logic.

STEP 0: CLE@TOR — TOR won by 22 (BLOWOUT). Mitchell on the losing side. STEP 1: Mitchell played 33 minutes, scored 15 PTS, recorded 3 AST — active. STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; no injury event. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'H2H 4/4 at T4 vs TOR; early-series AST safe per playoff profile.' The playoff profile 'AST safe early-series' guidance did not account for blowout game-script suppressing distribution in a road loss. With CLE down by a large margin, Mitchell shifted to pure scoring-try mode and his assist generation was limited to 3 (miss by 1). STEP 4: hit_rate_display 8/10, VOLATILE tag, trend=down, vs_tough=77%. Analyst correctly reduced to 70%. STEP 5: Tier walk showed T4 as the target after VOLATILE+T6 block. At 70% this was at the minimum floor — a marginal pick. STEP 6: lineup_update direction=unchanged. The model gap rule: the analyst applied the VOLATILE penalty and trend=down caution but did not apply a blowout-game-script AST suppression rule. When the game turns into a 22-point road blowout loss, the losing team's primary scorer enters isolation-rescue mode and AST volume drops predictably. Rule fix: apply a mandatory blowout_losing_side AST cap (max T2, confidence cap at 70%) when pre-game spread suggests possible blowout and the road team is a meaningful underdog.

STEP 0: DEN@MIN — MIN won by 17 (COMPETITIVE, borderline blowout). Jokic on the losing side. STEP 1: Post-game news confirms Jokic played 35 minutes, recorded 3 AST — active, no injury. STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; no injury event. Earlier lineup_update (Gordon OUT) revised down to 67%; later lineup_update (Jokic OUT — incorrect, he played) also fired. The 'Jokic OUT' lineup trigger appears to have been a false alarm since he played 35 minutes. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'H2H 6/6 perfect at T8 vs MIN; VOLATILE penalty absorbed by elite playmaker qualifier; mid opp helps.' The H2H perfect record (6/6) was compelling but represented regular-season data; in a playoff blowout loss (DEN lost by 17), Jokic's assist opportunities were compressed as the game got away from DEN and MIN's switching defense disrupted his playmaking sequences. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 8/10. The MIN×AST suppressor was noted and the VOLATILE+T8 penalty (-8pp for raw_avgs AST ≥ 6) was applied. Despite all penalties, the pick ran at 70% — the minimum floor. STEP 5: Tier walk correctly evaluated T10 (below floor), then T8 with all penalties applied, landing at exactly 70% grace zone. The pick was at minimum confidence. STEP 6: lineup_update direction=down (Gordon OUT, revised to 67%) — Amendment correctly flagged downside risk and revised below the prop-type floor. The pick ran despite the amendment dropping confidence below 68%. Amendment correctly flagged: Gordon OUT reduced dump-off targets, revised down to 67% — pick still ran and missed. Rule fix: when lineup_update direction=down revises AST confidence below the 68% floor, auto-void the AST pick. This is the clearest actionable fix from this miss.

STEP 0: DEN@MIN — MIN won by 17 (COMPETITIVE, borderline blowout). Murray on the losing side. STEP 1: Murray played 35 minutes, scored 16 PTS — active, no injury (confirmed post-game news). STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; no injury event. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited 'FG_MARGIN step from T25; series 2/2 at T30 and H2H 4/4 at T20 validate floor; road advantage applies.' The road advantage note was a positive signal but was overwhelmed by DEN losing badly (17 points). Post-game confirms the blowout script limited his second-half production. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 8/10 overall. FG_COLD:-9% and FG_MARGIN_THIN:1% were both flagged — these were active risk signals at pick time. STEP 5: Tier walk started at T25, stepped to T20 on FG_MARGIN. FG_COLD was noted but classified as 'below 15% threshold' for a second step-down — this threshold may be too permissive. STEP 6: lineup_update direction=up (Jokic OUT → revised to 85%). Amendment flagged upside (revised to 85%) but pick missed. The amendment's upside projection was incorrect because it did not account for the game becoming a 17-point loss where Murray's second-half usage was garbage-time compressed. Model gap rule: the system should not upgrade PTS confidence for absence-driven redistribution when FG_COLD is already active — the FG_COLD signal is a player-level shooting risk that is not offset by increased usage when the team is losing badly. Rule fix: when FG_COLD is flagged AND the team is on the road as an underdog, cap PTS confidence at original pre-amendment level regardless of absence-driven upside revisions.

STEP 0: DEN@MIN — MIN won by 17 (COMPETITIVE, borderline blowout). Johnson on the losing side. STEP 1: Johnson played 24 minutes (confirmed post-game news), went 0-for-3 from three. Active but limited minutes. STEP 2: NOT_LISTED at pick time; no injury event. Post-game confirms 24 minutes played — lower than projected 32 minutes. STEP 3: The analyst's reasoning cited '9/10 triggers trend=down T1 exception; MIN 3PM amplifier confirmed and vs_tough=90% support floor.' The reasoning correctly applied the 9/10 exception for trend=down and noted the MIN×3PM amplifier. However, Johnson played only 24 minutes (vs. projected 32) in a blowout loss — reduced minutes for bench players in a losing blowout compressed his 3PA attempts. He went 0-for-3 (3 attempts in 24 minutes) — the attempts were there but the makes were not. STEP 4: hit_rate_display 9/10 (90% overall) — this is a genuine high-rate miss. The trend=down signal had the exception correctly applied. STEP 5: Tier walk held at T1 via 9/10 exception. STEP 6: lineup_update direction=up (Jokic OUT → revised to 82%). Amendment flagged upside (revised to 82%) but pick missed. In a 17-point blowout loss, Johnson's shot creation was limited and the 3PM output was 0 despite 3 attempts. Model gap rule: for non-iron-floor 3PM T1 picks when trend=down is active (even with 9/10 exception), the game being a blowout loss for the player's team should apply a mandatory confidence cap reduction. Blowout losses compress catch-and-shoot 3PM opportunities for role players as the team stops executing its normal half-court sets in the second half. Human verdict correctly flagged 'trim' for this reason.

parlay_results.parlay_summary: 0/9 parlays — all nine cards missed; the three recurring killers were Nickeil Alexander-Walker PTS T15 (missed by 1 point, appeared in 7 of 9 parlays), Mikal Bridges 3PM T1 (0 threes in 21 minutes, appeared in 6 parlays), and Jamal Murray PTS T20 (16 pts in blowout loss, appeared in 5 parlays); the two Degen parlays were additionally sunk by Donovan Mitchell AST T4 and Jokic AST T8 misses; Degen #1 and #2 also included the Immanuel Quickley PTS leg which returned null (DNP) and was graded as a miss leg contributing to parlay failure.

reinforcements: Role-purity PTS picks at stepped-down tiers hit 100%: every FG_MARGIN_THIN or FG_MARGIN_NEG step-down from T15→T10 resulted in a HIT with enormous cushion (Castle 33 actual vs T10, Thompson 26 actual vs T10, Smith 24 actual vs T10, Fox 18 actual vs T10). The tier-step discipline is adding value — stepped-down tiers are not conservative misses, they are sound floor identification.

Primary PG assist floors were structurally sound across all three games. Fox AST T4 (6 actual), Thompson AST T4 (4 actual — exact threshold HIT), Castle AST T4 (5 actual), Tatum AST T4 (7 actual), White AST T2 (2 actual — exact threshold HIT), Edgecombe AST T2 (5 actual). The AST prop type went 6/6 (100%) validating the playmaker-role framework for identifying reliable assist floors.

3PM picks at T1 hit 100% across all three games and five picks. Vassell 3 actual vs T1, Fox 1 actual vs T1, Brown 1 actual vs T1, Tatum 5 actual vs T1 (iron_floor validated despite trend=down step), Smith 6 actual vs T2. The conservative T1 selection discipline for players with raw_avg near 1.5–2.0 is the correct approach — every pick cleared with margin.

Durant's absence (confirmed OUT pre-game) was correctly handled by the lineup update agent. Thompson PTS (26 actual vs T10), Smith PTS (24 actual vs T10), Smith REB (6 actual vs T4), and Smith 3PM (6 actual vs T2) all hit convincingly with KD's usage redistributed. The lineup_update direction='up' calls for Thompson and Smith were accurate — the agent correctly identified the usage cascade.

The PHI suppressor signal did not override sound BOS player picks in a competitive (BOS won by 8) game script. Brown PTS T20 (25 actual), Brown 3PM T1 (1 actual), Tatum AST T4 (7 actual), Tatum 3PM T1 (5 actual), White AST T2 (2 actual), Queta REB T4 (7 actual), Maxey PTS T15 (31 actual), Edgecombe PTS T10 (10 actual — exact threshold HIT), Edgecombe AST T2 (5 actual), George PTS T10 (18 actual) all hit. Correctly picking at stepped-down tiers while acknowledging the suppressor without hard-skipping was the right calibration.
lessons: REB is the structural weakness of this slate: 3 misses came from 3 different REB picks (Paul George REB T4: 0 actual, Jayson Tatum REB T8: 5 actual, Stephon Castle REB T4: 2 actual). All three players had non-zero minutes (41, 42, 34 respectively) and were not injury-limited. REB outcomes are more volatile game-to-game than PTS or AST, and the 57.1% REB hit rate on this slate (4/7) should prompt structural review of REB tier selection discipline.

Tatum REB T8 miss reveals that the 'vs_tough=60%' and 'competitive=57%' signals at T8 were meaningful warnings that were insufficiently weighted. The tier_walk acknowledged these as 'concerning' but still emitted at 72% exactly at the REB minimum floor — this is the weakest possible confidence for a pick and should trigger a skip or lower-tier evaluation. When REB vs_tough and competitive rates are both below 65% at the selected tier, the pick is borderline at best.

Paul George REB T4 with raw_avg_REB of 3.8 (below the T4 threshold) and actual output of 0 rebounds in 41 minutes is a clear signal: picking REB from below the player's average creates thin distributional support. The trim note flagged this correctly ('raw_avg_REB of 3.8 is below the T4 threshold') but the pick still ran. When raw_avg is below pick_value, the pick is already in the tail of the distribution — REB props should require raw_avg strictly above pick_value, not at or below it.

Castle REB T4 miss (2 actual in 34 minutes) occurred in a COMPETITIVE game (SAS won by 12) — not a blowout, not a late-game coasting situation. The VOLATILE tag on Castle REB was applied (-3%) but the underlying 80% base was driven by only 2 series games. Small series samples (n=2) should not anchor REB confidence at 80% when the VOLATILE tag is also present; the combination should produce a more significant reduction or a skip.

The human_verdict='trim' flags on REB picks (George, Tatum, Queta) correctly identified the structural fragility of these picks. The trim rationale on George REB ('raw_avg_REB of 3.8 is below the T4 threshold, post_miss_hr for REB is only 50%') and Tatum REB ('VOLATILE on REB, PHI def_recency tightened') were accurate pre-game warnings. The system's trim/manual_skip verdicts are better-calibrated than the base picks on REB props — when the human reviewer trims a REB pick, that signal should be treated as a near-skip.
recommendations: Implement a hard rule: REB picks require raw_avg_REB strictly greater than pick_value (not equal to or below). Paul George's raw_avg of 3.8 below the T4 threshold and Castle's thin distributional support at T4 are prototype cases. When raw_avg_REB < pick_value, classify as selection_error risk and skip unless iron_floor is confirmed.

When REB vs_tough AND competitive rates are both below 65% at the selected tier, require either (a) a tier step-down, or (b) 9/10 hit rate at the selected tier before emitting. Tatum's REB T8 had both signals flagged in the tier_walk as 'concerning' — the 72% minimum floor emission should not be allowed when two independent context rates both signal sub-65%. Treat dual-signal degradation as a skip trigger, not a 72% floor.

Apply a small-series-sample penalty to REB confidence when n_series ≤ 2 and VOLATILE is tagged. Castle's REB T4 was anchored on 2 series games at 80% base with a VOLATILE tag — this combination should cap at 68% maximum (below the 72% REB floor), effectively triggering a skip. Series samples of 2 are insufficient to override VOLATILE-tagged REB props at minimum tiers.

Treat human_verdict='trim' on REB picks as a de facto confidence ceiling of 70% for audit purposes. Across this slate, every trimmed REB pick either missed or hit marginally (Queta hit with 7 actual but was correctly identified as minutes-fragile). Establish a workflow rule: if the pick qualifies for trim based on the trim_reasons criteria (raw_avg below threshold, VOLATILE + blowout risk, min_floor near-zero), cap the emitted confidence at 70% and flag for manual review before including in parlays.

For the BOS vs PHI series specifically: do not fade BOS players purely on the PHI suppressor when opp_defense is rated 'mid' and the series H2H already shows 2/2 hits at the selected tier. The suppressor is real in competitive games but the within-series hit rate data should be weighted more heavily than the full-season suppressor signal after G2+. Continue applying opp_today=mid as the operative signal when series-level data contradicts the full-season suppressor, and reserve the suppressor penalty for first-game picks in the series where no within-series data exists.

miss_details.root_cause: STEP 0: BOS @ PHI finished 108-100 (CLOSE, margin 8). No blowout game script — George played 41 full minutes with no load management. STEP 1: George recorded non-zero stats (18 PTS, 5 AST per post-game news), confirming full activity. The 0 REB actual is a legitimate statistical output, not a DNP or injury exit. STEP 2: No injury_exit in post-game news. George played 41 minutes. POST-GAME NEWS confirms: 'George finished with zero rebounds in 41 minutes, badly missing the over 4 line. No injury exit or restriction was reported; he simply had an unusually poor rebounding game focused on perimeter play.' Classification: variance — the pick was at 72% (minimum REB floor), acknowledging the structural thinness. However, the hit_rate_display of 7/10 and the consistent tag were reasonable supports. STEP 3: The original reasoning cited 'Consistent tag, playoff REB +2.8 lift, 2/2 series H2H, strong stretch role' — but the trim note correctly identified that 'raw_avg_REB of 3.8 is below the T4 threshold,' meaning the pick was already in the tail of the distribution. A 0-rebound game from a perimeter-focused wing is within normal variance range but is amplified by picking from below the player's average. STEP 4: 7/10 hit rate — a miss at this rate is within expected variance (30% miss rate). STEP 5: Tier walk confirmed T4 as the only viable tier; T6 was not evaluated at ≥70% overall. The pick was at the minimum viable confidence. No tier_skip_error or data_conflict. STEP 6: No lineup_update present for George. Key structural note: raw_avg_REB below pick_value creates a thin distribution, and a 0-rebound game while playing perimeter-oriented minutes in a competitive close game is a legitimate statistical outcome. This is a variance miss, but one on a structurally fragile pick that the trim annotation correctly identified pre-game.

STEP 0: BOS @ PHI finished 108-100 (CLOSE, margin 8). No blowout context — Tatum played 42 full minutes. STEP 1: Post-game news confirms Tatum played 42 minutes with 5 REB actual (well below T8). Multiple non-zero stats confirmed — this is an active game miss. STEP 2: No injury_exit in post-game news. Web recap notes Tatum 'was still ramping up' from his ruptured Achilles suffered in the 2025 playoffs, which 'may have limited his physical aggression on the boards,' though no mid-game exit or restriction was flagged. The ramp-up context from the post-game news is meaningful — Tatum returned only in March 2026 from Achilles surgery. Classification: model_gap_rule — the signal existed (Achilles recovery ramp-up context from SEASON CONTEXT doc, VOLATILE tag, vs_tough=60% and competitive=57% at T8 both flagged in tier_walk as 'concerning') but the rule threshold at 72% minimum floor was too permissive to trigger a skip. The combination of VOLATILE + sub-65% contextual rates + Achilles recovery should have mandated a tier step-down or skip. STEP 3: The original reasoning cited '75% overall, 2/2 series at T8, 12.5 REB series avg, facilitator role maximizes boards' — this was directionally reasonable but ignored the physical-aggression dimension of rebounding for a player recovering from Achilles surgery. Achilles recovery typically limits explosive jumping and aggressive board-crashing, which is precisely the mechanism for a below-average rebounding game even in full minutes. The tier_walk acknowledged vs_tough=60% and competitive=57% as 'concerning' but still emitted at 72% exactly at the REB minimum floor. STEP 4: 7/10 hit rate at T8 — the miss is within the expected 25-30% miss range, but the contextual signals made this a structurally fragile pick. STEP 5: Tier walk evaluated T10 (not at ≥70% overall) and T8 (75% overall, confirmed). No higher-tier tier_skip_error. The single-tier evaluation at the margin (72% exactly) is an insufficient_walk flag — when the emitted confidence is exactly at the REB minimum floor, additional downside signals (VOLATILE, sub-65% contextual rates, Achilles ramp-up) should push below the floor and trigger a skip rather than a floor emission. STEP 6: No lineup_update present. Rule gap: dual-signal degradation (vs_tough AND competitive both <65% at selected tier) should trigger a mandatory tier step-down or skip — if no lower tier is viable, the pick should be dropped. The Achilles recovery context also represents a model_gap_rule: the system has no explicit rule penalizing REB props for players recovering from lower-body surgeries that affect explosive jumping within their return season.

STEP 0: SAS @ POR finished 120-108 (COMPETITIVE, margin 12). Not a blowout — no game-script compression excuse. Castle played 34 minutes, a reasonable allocation in a competitive road win. STEP 1: Post-game news confirms Castle played 34 minutes with 2 REB actual. Non-zero stats in other categories confirmed (33 PTS per Castle PTS HIT). This is a genuine rebounding miss in full minutes. STEP 2: No injury_exit in post-game news. Web recap notes 'no explicit mid-game exit or restriction is noted for this game; the miss appears to be a low-output rebounding performance.' Classification: variance — the pick was at 72% (minimum REB floor) with VOLATILE tag applied and a 7/10 hit rate acknowledging the expected miss rate. STEP 3: The original reasoning cited '80% overall, 3/3 H2H, VOLATILE -3% applied; Wemby absence amplifies role' — the Wemby absence argument was directionally reasonable (expanded offensive role), but did not necessarily translate to expanded rebounding role; with Wemby out, Castle's primary role expansion was as a ball-handler and scorer (he scored 33), not as a rebounder. The absence of a rim-protecting center may have actually reduced the total number of defensive rebound opportunities for perimeter players by changing shot quality. STEP 4: 8/10 hit rate is solid, but this is a T4 REB pick with VOLATILE tag — the 20% miss rate is real. A 2-rebound game from a guard in 34 minutes in a competitive game is within normal variance. STEP 5: Tier walk evaluated T6 (not confirmed at ≥70% per quant due to small H2H sample) and T4 (80% overall confirmed). Emitted at T4 — only one viable tier evaluated, but the minimum-tier floor emission at 72% with VOLATILE is the correct output given the data. No tier_skip_error. STEP 6: No lineup_update present for Castle REB. The human_verdict='keep' was reasonable given the quant support, but the VOLATILE tag on a 2-game series sample anchoring the 80% base reflects the core fragility of this pick. A 2-rebound game from a ball-handling guard who is primarily a scorer/playmaker (not a natural rebounder) in a competitive away game is a legitimate statistical variance event at this tier and confidence level.

reinforcements: Tier step-down cascade discipline held across the full slate: every FG_MARGIN_NEG, FG_MARGIN_THIN, and FG_COLD mandatory step fired correctly and all stepped-down picks hit comfortably (Murray PTS T15 hit 30, Brunson PTS T15 hit 19, Randle PTS T10 hit 15, Bane PTS T10 hit 25). The rule's conservatism was validated without exception.

The MIN×AST suppressor step-down on Jokic AST (T10→T6) was correctly applied and the pick hit 9 AST — the suppressor caution preserved a live pick that would have been marginal at T10 given G3's 3-AST outcome, confirming suppressor-driven tier steps add protective value without eliminating strong underlying signals.

Playoff dossier STRONG_ELEVATOR treatment for Banchero paid off: PTS T15 hit 25 and REB T4 hit 12. Despite human trim flags on both, the analyst's core logic of recognizing Banchero's playoff elevation profile was structurally correct — the tier step-downs applied (FG_MARGIN_THIN on PTS) were appropriately conservative while still generating hits.

SGA T20 structural-lock directive was validated for the third consecutive game: scored 42 on a competitive PHX road game, delivering 22 points above threshold. The directive to avoid evaluating T20 and begin at T25 saved tokens and the FG_MARGIN_NEG step from T25→T20 correctly identified T20 as the safe anchor.

Jokic triple-coverage (PTS/REB/AST) all hit in a must-win DEN home game: PTS 24 vs T20, REB 15 vs T10, AST 9 vs T6. The layered risk management — iron_floor on PTS, 25th-pct gate step on REB, MIN×AST suppressor step on AST — produced a clean 3/3 outcome on the highest-volume player on the slate, confirming that conservative tier selection on an elite player in a competitive game context is a reliable strategy.
lessons: Anthony Edwards AST T2 miss (0 actual): the pick was undone by an in-game left knee injury exit at 18 minutes, not by analytical error. The analyst's concern about G3 load management (24 min) was directionally correct as a risk signal, but the pre-pick confidence of 82% appropriately reflected a structurally sound T2 pick given the injury_status was NOT_LISTED at check time. The injury event cannot be predicted from available data; however, the prior-game minutes reduction pattern (24 min in G3) was a documented signal in the pick object and the analyst noted it — this represents appropriate risk acknowledgment on a pick that still warranted selection. No lesson applicable to selection logic: miss is an injury_event.

The 81-85% confidence band registered 50% actual hit rate (1/2) against an 83% expectation — both picks in this band were Murray AST T4 (hit) and Edwards AST T2 (miss via injury). The band underperformance is entirely attributable to the injury event, not to miscalibration. No recalibration is warranted.

The human_verdict 'manual_skip' applied to OG Anunoby REB T6 (citing raw_avg 5.7 below pick threshold) was overridden by the actual outcome of 10 rebounds — the strongest blowout-side REB overage on the slate. The NYK win by 16 in Atlanta created a game script where Anunoby played 40 minutes at full load, generating well above the T6 threshold. The raw_avg-below-threshold skip rule may be overcautious for reliable rebounders in competitive playoff games where minutes are guaranteed.
recommendations: Establish a formal minutes-reduction carry-forward rule for in-series AST picks: when a player has had their minutes actively managed down by ≥10 minutes vs. their season average in the immediately preceding game (e.g., Edwards G3: 24 min vs. 38-min season avg), apply a mandatory -5% confidence penalty to ALL counting stat picks for that player in the next game, even at minimum tiers like T2. This bridges the gap between the analyst's acknowledged concern and a quantified rule adjustment.

Reconsider the raw_avg-below-threshold skip criterion for REB on wing players (SF/PF) in playoff games where minutes are structurally locked (≥38 avg in series, no injury flag, competitive game): Anunoby's 5.7 raw_avg vs. T6 threshold triggered a manual_skip that missed a 10-REB actual. A more precise rule would be: skip only when raw_avg is more than 1.5 units below pick_value AND the player carries a VOLATILE tag — a non-VOLATILE player with an 8/10 hit rate and a series avg near the threshold warrants selection even below raw_avg.

For the DEN vs. MIN series going forward: Jokic's AST suppression by the MIN×AST scheme is now confirmed across G3 (3 AST in blowout) and G4 (9 AST in competitive home game). The signal behaves directionally as predicted — blowout game scripts suppress Jokic AST (3 in a 17-pt loss) while competitive must-win contexts allow full playmaking (9 in a close game). Apply the MIN×AST step-down advisory specifically when game context is BLOWOUT or DEN is trailing; do NOT apply it when DEN is in a must-win home context with a competitive spread (±3 or less).

Anthony Edwards' knee is now a confirmed mid-game exit risk — left knee injury forced him out of G4 with 18 min played. For any Edwards prop pick in G5 (if series continues), require injury_status = NOT_LISTED AND a confirmed pre-game workout or shoot-around participation before including him. If listed GTD or QUESTIONABLE, void automatically. The G3 24-min load management followed by G4 injury exit creates a two-game pattern that warrants a mandatory injury-status gate independent of confidence.

The 70-75% band dramatically over-performed (100% actual vs. 72.5% expected) across 14 picks. While single-day samples are noisy, the consistent over-performance of conservative minimum-tier picks (T4/T6/T10 stepped down from higher tiers) across multiple sessions suggests the step-down cascade is generating alpha at lower confidence thresholds. Consider whether the 68% minimum floor is too low as a gate — the system's real floor on well-reasoned stepped-down picks may be closer to 75-78%, meaning picks that calculate to 68-72% after all penalties are being under-priced relative to their actual performance. Monitor this band across the next 5 sessions before adjusting the floor upward.

miss_details.root_cause: STEP 0 — Game context: DEN @ MIN, MIN won 112-96 (margin 16, COMPETITIVE per game results). STEP 1 — Activity check: actual_value = 0 AST. The analyst did note G3's 24-minute load management as a risk factor and applied a conservative 82% confidence accordingly. The reasoning correctly identified the minutes risk but could not anticipate an acute in-game knee injury. STEP 5 — Tier walk: T2 was the only evaluated tier; this was appropriate given T2 is the minimum AST tier and the gate (T4+) did not apply. No tier_skip_error or data_conflict. Player was active at pick time (NOT_LISTED); miss is entirely attributable to an unpredictable acute injury event, not to pick selection logic.

reinforcements: AST floor picks on primary playmakers with H2H perfect records held cleanly: Harden AST T4 (8/10 base, H2H 3/3, series 3/3) hit 8.0 actual vs 4 threshold; Fox AST T4 (H2H 6/6, series 3/3) hit 7.0 actual — the dossier 'bulletproof mid-series' annotation and vs_tough=91% confirmed as reliable structural anchors even in road blowout contexts.

PTS picks on teams' primary offensive engines in both blowout wins and competitive games performed well above tier: Barnes PTS T15 hit 23.0, Tatum PTS T20 hit 30.0, Fox PTS T15 hit 28.0, Castle PTS T10 hit 16.0, Ingram PTS T10 hit 23.0 — demonstrating that conservative tier-walking to T10/T15 on primary scorers produced clean hits with significant cushion, validating the FG_MARGIN step-down protocol.

3PM picks went 6/6 (100%) across the slate, including all tier-walk step-downs applied (Brown T2→T1, Tatum T2→T1 via trend=down, Fox T1 via VOLATILE floor, Vassell T1 via iron_floor) — the combination of dossier-driven step-downs and non-iron-floor 80% cap discipline produced a perfect prop-type day, confirming the conservative 3PM tier-walk rules are well-calibrated for playoff contexts.

Blowout context (BOS@PHI 128-96, SAS@POR 114-93) did not materially harm picks on winning-side primary scorers — Tatum 30 PTS, Brown 3 3PM, Fox 28 PTS, Castle 16 PTS, Vassell 1 3PM all hit — confirming that stars on the winning side in blowouts still clear conservative low-tier thresholds even with garbage-time minute reductions.

The lineup amendment correctly flagged upside on Sengun PTS (revised to 91%), Amen Thompson PTS (revised to 83%), and Jabari Smith REB (unchanged at 85%) after Durant OUT — all three hit — validating the afternoon agent's Durant-absence redistribution logic; and the Bona-out amendment correctly kept Maxey PTS/3PM unchanged, which both hit comfortably.
lessons: RJ Barrett AST T2 missed (actual 1.0) in a CLOSE game (TOR won by 4): the human_verdict was 'keep' and the market line of 1.5 indicated the market priced this close to the threshold. Barrett recorded only 1 assist despite 39 minutes played — TOR's offense ran through Barnes as primary facilitator (6 AST per post-game news), directly compressing Barrett's playmaking role. The H2H 5/5 at T2 perfect record was against regular-season CLE, but the playoff series context differs: Barnes's expanded role as the offensive initiator is a structural factor the system's H2H window did not capture. The trend=down tag was informational but not penalized — in future, trend=down on AST for a secondary creator in a series where a teammate has taken over facilitation duties should trigger a mandatory step or skip consideration.

Alperen Sengun REB T8 missed (actual 6.0, threshold 8): game context was COMPETITIVE (HOU won by 19, technically above the 10-point COMPETITIVE threshold at 19 points — borderline COMPETITIVE/BLOWOUT). Per post-game web recap, Sengun grabbed only 6 defensive boards against LAL's frontcourt. The VOLATILE tag fired and confidence was only 72%, within normal variance range. However, the raw_avg of 8.4 sits barely above T8, and the LAL suppressor environment combined with the sweep-game script (losing team eliminating risk psychology was less present since HOU was winning) reduced second-chance opportunities. The trim reason correctly identified LAL def_recency at -12.2% as a structural risk — the miss validates that FG_COLD at -14% (just below the 15% mandatory step) should have prompted a voluntary step-down from T8 to T6 when the opp_defense is simultaneously flagged as tightening. Amendment flagged upside (revised to 77%) but pick missed.

Dylan Harper AST T2 missed (actual 1.0) in a blowout win (SAS won by 21): post-game news confirms Harper played 25 minutes in a blowout and managed only 1 assist, with Fox and Castle handling primary playmaking. The blowout game script on the winning side compresses bench players' role in late-game situations — Harper's 25-minute floor in a comfortable win meant his assist opportunities were structurally limited by game script, not just role compression. The Wemby QUES penalty at -5% was applied but the blowout risk was not explicitly computed as an additional penalty. For non-primary-creator AST picks on blowout-side bench players, the blowout game script should apply an additional deduction even when spread_abs was below the 11-point playoff threshold pre-game — the series diary showed SAS winning G3 by 12 on the road, establishing a pattern of SAS control that should have informed blowout probability.

Brandon Ingram AST T2 missed (actual 0.0) despite H2H 6/6 perfect record: the post-game web recap confirms 0 assists in 36 minutes as TOR's offense ran through Barnes and Barrett, not Ingram. The human_verdict was 'trim' with the trim note correctly identifying post_miss_hr of 67% as the worst bounce-back rate on the slate. This is a sequential miss pattern (series 1/3 at T4 already established underperformance) where the H2H anchor was from regular-season games before Barnes/Barrett had assumed their current series roles. The lesson: in playoff series where a player's role has clearly compressed across multiple consecutive games (series 1/3 at T4, series context shows Barnes as facilitator), regular-season H2H perfect records at T2 should be discounted more aggressively — the 75% confidence was appropriate but the pick should have been skipped given the structural role evidence already in the series diary.

VJ Edgecombe PTS T10 missed (actual 6.0) in a blowout loss (BOS won by 32): post-game news confirms 2-9 FG, 0-4 from three in a 32-point loss. The BOS@PHI game was a BLOWOUT (margin 32), and PHI as the losing side faced starters-benched-in-Q4 and disrupted offensive rhythm. The blowout_secondary_scorer rule was noted but not applied because spread_abs=7.5 was below the 16.5 playoff threshold — however, the actual game played out as a historic 32-point blowout, far exceeding the pre-game spread. The key gap: FG_COLD at -10% was below the 15% mandatory step threshold, but the PHI suppressor environment (confirmed system-wide -22.1pp) combined with BOS's defensive dominance at full strength created a compounding suppression effect on PHI's secondary scorers that the system did not fully price in. Edgecombe's series production of 1/3 at T15 already signaled instability at higher tiers; the 85% base rate was anchored on the broader window without adequate series-level penalty.

VJ Edgecombe AST T2 missed (actual 1.0): the iron_floor was correctly downgraded to soft_floor via the role-purity check (PTS/AST ratio 5.4 > 3x). However, the blowout context (BOS won by 32) is the dominant causal factor — in a 32-point road loss, PHI's secondary creator AST opportunities collapse structurally regardless of player-level floors. The market priced this at 93.55% implied probability (market line 1.5), indicating the market also expected a floor hit — both the system and market were wrong due to the severity of the blowout, which created a game-script suppression that no pre-game signal fully priced. The soft_floor downgrade was the correct move, but 75% confidence on a secondary creator AST in a game where the team's entire offensive system could collapse under defensive pressure warrants a harder skip at the pre-game analysis stage when both the PHI suppressor and BOS's confirmed defensive superiority are present together.
recommendations: Implement a secondary-creator AST series-role audit: before accepting H2H records at T2 for non-primary creators (raw_avgs AST < 4.0), check the current series diary for evidence that a teammate has assumed facilitation duties. Barrett's H2H 5/5 was voided by Barnes becoming TOR's primary facilitator mid-series; Ingram's H2H 6/6 was voided by the same dynamic. Add a rule: if the series diary or game log shows a teammate generating materially more AST than their season average in the last 2 games, apply an additional -5pp to secondary creator AST picks regardless of H2H record.

Apply a voluntary FG_COLD step-down when FG_COLD is between -12% and -14% AND opp_defense is simultaneously flagged as 'tough' or 'tightening' (def_recency delta negative): the current rule requires -15% for a mandatory step, but Sengun REB T8 missed in exactly this scenario (FG_COLD -14%, LAL def_recency -12.2%). The combined signal — near-threshold cold shooting against a tightening defense — should trigger a voluntary step-down one tier as a risk management rule, even without crossing the -15% mandatory threshold.

Strengthen blowout probability pricing for AST and low-tier scoring props on bench/secondary players in games where both a confirmed system-wide suppressor is active (PHI) and the opponent is at full strength: the Edgecombe PTS/AST misses both occurred in the PHI-suppressor environment with BOS fully healthy. The pre-game spread of 7.5 understated the blowout risk in this specific structural combination. Add a rule: when the picking team is a confirmed system-wide suppressor (PHI, HOU, LAL) AND faces an opponent at full strength, apply an additional -5pp to secondary scorer PTS and AST picks regardless of spread_abs — the suppressor mechanism implies game-script risk beyond what spread alone captures.

For bench players with average minutes between 20-26 in blowout-likely scenarios on the winning side (Harper AST T2, projected in a series where SAS had won G3 by 12 on the road), apply a blowout game-script AST discount of -5pp even when the pre-game spread is below the formal threshold. The series diary context (SAS leading 3-0, prior road win by 12) should feed a blowout probability estimate that overrides the raw spread_abs threshold; specifically, when the series diary shows the picking team won their most recent road game by 10+, treat the current road game as having elevated blowout probability and reduce confidence on bench-player AST props accordingly.

Recalibrate the 70-75% confidence band: actual hit rate 69.2% is 3.3pp below expected 72.5%, and all 4 AST misses (Barrett, Harper, Ingram, Edgecombe) plus the Sengun REB miss fell in this band. The 70-75% band is systematically producing results below its stated probability, suggesting these are marginal picks that should either be stepped down further or skipped. Implement a rule: no AST pick below 76% confidence in a playoff blowout-risk game (spread_abs > 6 or series diary showing prior blowout), and treat 70-72% AST picks as skip candidates unless iron_floor is confirmed and series data is 3/3 at the selected tier.

The actual game was not a desperation game for HOU (they won by 19), which may have reduced Sengun's rebounding motivation in garbage time. STEP 4: Hit rate 7/10 at T6/T8 level with VOLATILE tag — 72% confidence reflects that this was a borderline pick. A 7/10 prop misses 30% of the time by definition. STEP 5: T2 was the correct tier (T4 series 0/3 correctly rejected). The walk was appropriate. STEP 6: No lineup amendment present. A rule computing blowout probability from series context (prior winning margin on road) and applying a deduction to bench-player AST regardless of raw spread would have caught this.
